title: Poa berteroniana
taxonomy: Poa berteroniana is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and is native to the Pacific Islands. Poa berteroniana is further classified into the genus Poa and the subtribe Poeinae. This species of grass is often found in high-altitude areas with grassland or rocky terrain. Its classification also places it within the subfamily Pooideae.

intro: Poa berteroniana, also known as Poa, is a perennial grass species native to Hawaii and the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for soothing digestive issues, reducing pain, and protecting against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Poa berteroniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive problems and skin irritations.
